feed.title: Accessibility Wars - spear
feed.subtitle: Accessible Builds for the Guild Wars 2 Community

entry.title: Holy Nova [aHeal]
entry.content:
This is a very accessible build that relies on an excessive amount of healing, CC, condi cleanses and utilites, more than you will ever need. Ideally, the build should function as a low-toughness healer.

This build is low cognition, has very few repetitions and is functional in instanced content. Although the maximum APM of the build is quite high, you probably can survive without mashing keys due to how much healing is baked into the core guardian and Luminary kit. This build requires only one weapon with no requirement of a weapon swap although you must interact with the Luminary's Forge ability.

There is an excessive amount of light effects on this build and you may wish to adjust your graphics settings if the intensity is too high.

Gearing

Equipment

ArmorStatUpgrade
Harrier's Monk
Harrier's Monk
Harrier's Monk
Harrier's Monk
Harrier's Monk
Harrier's Monk
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Spear Harrier's Transference
Concentration
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Harrier's
Amulet Harrier's
Accessory Harrier's
Accessory Harrier's
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Harrier's
Ring Harrier's
Relic Karakosa

Alternate Pieces

WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Staff Harrier's Transference
Concentration
  •  Relic of the Flock
  •  Relic of Febe

Note: Weapon swap is optional. Alternate weapon sets should be prioritised based on utility requirements. Note: Febe gives patchy swiftness coverage but allows you to avoid swapping to staff for Symbol of Swiftness. Note: If you do not use these sources of swifness, you will need to take "Advance!".

Budget Options

  • Replace  Superior Rune of the Monk with  Superior Rune of Rebirth
  • Replace  Superior Sigil of Transference with  Superior Sigil of Water

Consumables

  •  Bowl of Fruit Salad with Mint Garnish OR  Delicious Rice Ball (budget alternative)
  •  Bountiful Maintenance Oil

Build

Heal Alacrity Build

[&DQExLS45URVhHTgBVx0tAXgdSAFWHTIBiRIAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAACMwAJAQA=]

Trait Alternatives

  • Bring Master of Consecrations if you are taking consecrations.
  • This build is a simplified version of the heal alacrity build on snowcrows with selective preferences for lower intensity. If you are able to adapt to the version on SC, feel free to follow their guide.

Rotation and Utilities

The excessive amount of healing provided by this build piles on top of the already flexible healing and support capabilties of the Core Guardian kit. Of note, Light's Gift causes Luminary's Blessing to heal allies whenever it is refreshed or expires.

You should not be adjusting the build to try to increase healing but focus on your utilities instead.

Heal and Alacrity Alac Rotation

  1. Use your virtues before Enter Radiant Forge
  2. Use skills 5-4-3-2 in Forge
    • to grant alacrity
    • for a strong CC
    • To burst heal
  3. Exit Radiant Forge
  4. Using any of your utilities will overcap on protection, which can be used on cooldown or reserved
    • Reserve Resolute Stance for Condi Cleanse and healing
    • Reserve Stalwart Stance for Aegis
    • Reserve Valorous Stance for Stability
    • Reserve Effulgent Stance for extra CC
    • Use "Feel My Wrath!" on cooldown for extra Fury uptime
  5. Use skills 5-4-3-2 with your spear
    • To grant Fury
    • For a minor CC
    • To burst heal
  6. Repeat step 1 when refreshed.
  • Note: If your might uptime is patchy, consider spamming Radiant Justice (F1) during your Forge rotation.

Crowd Control rotation

  1. You have decent CC using only Brilliant Slam (Forge 5 Flip)
  2. You can extend your CC rotation by starting with Dazzling Hammer (Forge 2) i.e. 2-5-4-3-2
  3. Use Effulgent Stance for extra CC
  4. Symbol of Luminance (Spear 5)
  5. Sanctuary (Alt Utility Skill)
  • Note: More options are available but not exhausted in this guide.

Condi Cleanses

  1. Radiant Resolve (F2)
  2. Restorative Glow (Forge 3 Flip)
  3. Resolute Stance (Heal)
  4. Solar Storm (Spear 4)
  5. Purging Flames (Alt Utility)
  6. Smite Condition (Alt Utility)
  • Note: It is very unlikely that you will require to use alternate utilities for extra condi cleanses.

Situational Utilties/Relics

  1. Wall of Reflection
  2. Hallowed Ground
  3. "Stand Your Ground!"
  4. "Advance!" (Swiftness)
  • Note: If you do not use Symbol of Swiftness (Staff 3) or  Relic of Febe, you will need alternate sources of swiftness like "Advance!".
  • Note: "Advance!" has to be used on cooldown for 100% swiftness coverage but you can rely on Radiant Courage as an alternate source of Aegis.

entry.title: 0-KIT ALAC-T1000-C [aDPS]
entry.content:
Introduction

This is build is equipped similar to the Snowcrow's guide but reduces the need for kits. The kits can be added back for additional DPS when one is comfortable with the build.

Gearing

Equipment

ArmorStatUpgrade
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
Viper's Trapper
WeaponTypeStatUpgrade
Spear Celestial Bursting
Earth
TrinketsTypeStat
Back item Celestial
Amulet Sinister
Accessory Celestial
Accessory Celestial
TrinketsTypeStat
Ring Viper's
Ring Viper's
Relic Fractal
  • Start by adding Viper/Celestial Pieces until your condi duration is about 67%. More celestial pieces will provide more concentration and boon duration of 12-30% is ideal. You can fill the remaining slots with Sinister or Grieving gear. The above gear has 16% boon duration.

Food and Utility and Infusions

  •  Cilantro and Cured Meat Flatbread
  •  Toxic Tuning Crystal
  •  Malign +5 Agony Infusion (optional)

Build

[&DQMGJyY5SyYqDwAAWh0AAIIdAAAiAQAAex0AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAACCQE2AAA=]

GW2Skills Link

Rotation

Granting Alacrity

You give Alacrity with Morph Skills:

  1. Offensive Protocol: Obliterate
  2. Offensive Protocol: Pierce
  3. Defensive Protocol: Thorns
  • You may replace Offensive Protocol: Pierce with another protocol of choice for additional utility.
  • You should use these skills spread out but frequently enough to ensure Alacrity doesn't drop.
  • Offensive Protocol: Obliterate and Defensive Protocol: Thorns are your main damage buffs.

DPS Rotation

  1. Precast Flame Turret and DO NOT Detonate Flame Turret
  2. Use all your skills off-cooldown except your CC ( Static Shock & Roiling Skies) and healing skill ( Static Shock)
  3. Spread out morph skills to maintain Willing Host

CC skills

  1. Static Shock (AED Toolbelt)
  2. Roiling Skies (Spear 4)
  3. Offensive Protocol: Pierce (used in DPS rotation)
  4. Flux State (used in DPS rotation)

Sustainability

  1. Liquid State is an evade stance
  2. A.E.D. can bypass many mechanics.
  3. Stainless Steel is useful for additional heals
  4. Innervating Alloy can be used for condi cleanses
  5.  Relic of the Biomancer or Mist Stranger for Open-world

Benchmark

The estimated DPS is provided without any infusions.

entry.title: Seven Paths of Pain [qDPS]
entry.content:
This build uses 7 key skills in its rotation without weapon swap. With only 2 short bow skills Sevenshot and Bloodbane Path, this build avoids high energy skills in Mallyx, allowing energy management to frequently trigger torment through Embrace the Darkness. The ranged nature of the build allows higher damage uptime, less button presses and the relics and sigils are focused on stacking as much torment as possible. Originally build for bow, the build has now been updated to include spear in its rotation.

Gearing and Consumables

Optimised Loadout GW2Skills Link

EquipmentStatsRune/Sigil/Relic
ArmorViper's Superior Rune of the Trapper
Short BowViper's Superior Sigil of Malice
 Superior Sigil of Torment
SpearViper's Superior Sigil of Malice
 Superior Sigil of Torment[1]
TrinketsViper's Relic of the Fractal

Short Bow/Spear and Spear/Spear are the most popular loadouts.

Weapon Alternatives

  1. Mace/Axe - This does more damage in theory than bow. However, in practice, the melee range may cause you to miss hits, thus swapping to bow/spear is useful. In light of the JW update, spears have now overshadowed mace.
  2. Staff - This may be used for large amounts of CC, but is detrimental to your DPS. This is to be used situationally only and is strongly not recommended.

Food

  •  Salsa-Topped Veggie Flatbread
  •  Koi Cake(budget alternative)

Utility

  •  Toxic Tuning Crystal

Build

[&DQkOHQMWNBbcEQAA1BEAACsSAAAGEgAAyhEAAAEEAAArEtQRBhIAAAAAAAACawAFAAA=]

Trait Alternatives

The equipment stats are fairly similar to the that of SnowCrows with selection of different sigils. If you would like to incorporate weapon swaps into your rotation and optimise your damage, refer to the SnowCrows guide.

Rotation

Generating Quickness in Glint

  1. Facet of Chaos (upkeep, precast)
  2. Facet of Darkness (upkeep, opener)

You will automatically generate quickness through Elevated Compassion

Consuming skills (on cooldown)

  1. Facet of Strength -> Burst of Strength
  2. Facet of Elements -> Elemental Blast (Ground Targeted) Swap to Legendary Demon Stance after consuming strength second time.

Generating Quickness in Mallyx

  1. Embrace the Darkness (upkeep) Swap to Legendary Dragon Stance when out of energy,

Weapon Rotation

Short Bow
  1. Bloodbane Path (Short Bow 2)
  2. Sevenshot (Short Bow 3) Use 2/3 off cooldown. If you end up having excess energy because you messed up your rotation, you can use Spiritcrush Bow 4. However if executed properly, this is a very energy hungry build and you should not have enough. If you are forced into range and may have damage downtime/narrow windows such that you cannot maintain stacks of Crushing Abyss, bow would be your default weapon. Bow also has a shorter ramp up time.
Spear
  1. Abyssal Force (Spear 2)
  2. Abyssal Raze (Spear 5) Use 2/5 off cooldown. Autos will lower the cooldown of Abyssal Raze, but also requires your attacks to hit. If there is a wide open damage window, spear is your weapon of choice. Legend swaps count as swaps for sigils but does not trigger Abyssal Raze. Spears offer the highest DPS at the time of writing.
Mace/Axe
  1. Searing Fissure (Mace 2)
  2. Echoing Eruption (Mace 3) Use 2/3 off cooldown. If you end up having excess energy because you messed up your rotation, you can use Temporal Rift (Axe 5). However if executed properly, this is a very energy hungry build and you should not have enough.


The above skills 1-5 occur in a loop while weapon skills are available on all stances and can be performed anytime.

Optional Support

True Nature (on cooldown) This skill is not necessary to upkeep quickness and is neglible to DPS. However it can extend other boons and is thus included to assist your healer.

Optimisation

CC

  1. Scorchrazor is always available (Short Bow 5)
  2. Abyssal Blot is always available (Spear 4)
  3. Temporal Rift is always available (Axe 5)
  4. Call to Anguish is available in Mallyx
  5. Chaotic Release is available in Glint You may bring a staff if you really need a strong CC

Sustain and Stunbreaks

  1. Gaze of Darkness is available in Glint
  2. This build heals through Elevated Compassion
  3. You have protection for yourself and your subgroup when in Glint

Videos

Coming Soon

  1. While it's possible to play this build solo, spear will not trigger enough bleeds in a solo environment to proc the relic.  Relic of the Aristocracy and  Relic of Akeem are possible alternatives. ↩
